Items.Title: Rails 的隐藏宝石：ActiveSupport 缓存增量和减量 | Rails' Hidden Gems: ActiveSupport Cache Increment and Decrement
Items.Description:
本文最初由Jonathan Miles在Honeybadger 开发人员博客上撰写。

在本系列中，我们将了解隐藏在 Rails 大型代码库中的一些鲜为人知的工具。

在本文中，我们将解释Rails.cache中的increment和decrement方法。

它也是一种抽象，为您提供一个通用的 API 来调用，而不管在后台使用的实际缓存“存储”如何。 开箱即用，Rails 支持以下内容：

  • 文件存储
  • 记忆库
  • 空存储
  • 检查Rails.cache将显示您正在运行哪一个：

     > Rails . cache => < #ActiveSupport::Cache::RedisCacheStore options={:namespace=>nil, ...

    我们不会详细查看它们，而是作为一个快速总结：

    • 从这里读回来总是返回nil 。 这是新 Rails 应用程序的默认设置。
    • 由于各种原因，这里的前三个最常用于开发/测试。 在生产中，您可能会使用 Redis 或 MemCache。

      因为Rails.cache抽象了这些服务之间的差异，所以很容易在不同的环境中运行不同的版本，而无需更改代码； 例如，您可以在开发中使用NullStore ，在测试中使用MemoryStore ，在生产中使用RedisCacheStore 。

      缓存数据

      通过 Rails 的Rails.cache.read 、 Rails.cache.write和Rails.cache.fetch ，我们可以轻松地从缓存中存储和检索任意数据。 较早的一篇文章更详细地介绍了这些； 本文要注意的重要一点是，这些方法没有内置的线程安全性。 假设我们正在更新来自多个线程的缓存数据以保持运行计数； 我们需要将读/写操作包装在某种锁中以避免竞争条件。 考虑这个例子，假设我们已经设置了使用 Redis 缓存存储：

       threads = [] # Set initial counter Rails . cache . write ( :test_counter , 0 ) 4 . times do threads << Thread . new do 100 . times do current_count = Rails . cache . read ( :test_counter ) current_count += 1 Rails . cache . write ( :test_counter , current_count ) end end end threads . map ( & :join ) puts Rails . cache . read ( :test_counter )

      这里我们有四个线程，每个线程将我们的缓存值增加一百次。 结果应该是 400，但大多数时候，它会少得多——在我的测试运行中是 269。 这里发生的是竞争条件。 我在上一篇文章中更详细地介绍了这些，但作为一个快速总结，因为线程都在相同的“共享”数据上运行，它们可能会彼此不同步。 例如，一个线程可能读取该值，然后另一个线程接管并读取该值，递增并存储它。 然后第一个线程使用它现在过期的值恢复。

      解决这个问题的常用方法是用互斥锁（或互斥锁）包围代码，这样每次只有一个线程可以执行锁内的代码。 然而，在我们的例子中，Rails.cache 有一些方法来处理这种情况。

      要使用increment和decrement ，我们必须告诉缓存存储它是一个“原始”值（通过raw: true ）。 您在读取值时也必须这样做； 否则，你会得到一个错误。 基本上，我们告诉缓存我们希望将此值存储为裸整数，以便我们可以对其调用递增/递减，但您仍然可以同时使用expires_in和其他缓存标志。

      这里的关键是increment和decrement使用原子操作（至少对于 Redis 和 MemCache 而言），这意味着它们是线程安全的； 在原子操作期间，线程无法暂停。

      值得注意的是，虽然我在这里的示例中没有使用它，但这两种方法也都返回了新值。 因此，如果您需要使用新的计数器值而不仅仅是更新它，您也可以这样做而无需额外的read调用。

      实际应用

      从表面上看，这些increment和decrement方法看起来像是低级的辅助方法，如果您正在实现或维护类似后台作业处理 gem 之类的东西，您可能只关心这种方法。 但是，一旦您了解它们，您可能会惊讶于它们在哪里可以派上用场。

      我在生产应用程序中使用了这些，以避免同时运行重复的预定后台作业。 在我们的例子中，我们有各种计划的作业来更新搜索索引、标记废弃的购物车等等。 一般来说，这很好用； 问题是某些工作（特别是搜索索引）会消耗大量内存 - 如果两个工作一起运行，它将超过我们 Heroku dyno 的限制，并且工人将被杀死。 因为我们有一些这样的工作，所以并不像将它们标记为不重试或强制独特的工作那么简单； 两个不同的（因此是独特的）作业可能会尝试同时运行并让工人失望。

      为了防止这种情况，我们为计划作业创建了一个基类，用于记录当前正在运行的作业数量。 如果计数太高，作业只会重新排队并等待。

      另一个例子是我的一个副项目，其中一个后台作业（或多个作业）在用户必须等待时进行一些处理。 这带来了将当前进度传达给后台作业用户的常见问题。 虽然有很多方法可以解决这个问题，但作为一个实验，我尝试使用Rails.cache.increment来更新全局可用的计数器。 结构如下：

      1. 首先，我在/app/models中添加了一个新类，以抽象出计数器存在于缓存中的事实。 这是对值的所有访问都将流经的地方。 其中一部分是生成与作业相关的唯一缓存键。
      2. 然后，该作业创建此模型的一个实例，并在处理项目时对其进行更新。
      3. 一个简单的 JSON 端点创建此模型的一个实例来获取当前值。
      4. 前端每隔几秒就会轮询此端点以更新 UI。 当然，您可以使用 ActionCable 之类的东西使这个功能更加出色并推出更新。

      结论

      老实说， Rails.cache.increment不是我经常使用的工具，因为我不经常想要更新存储在缓存中的数据（这本质上是暂时的）。 如上所述，我确实达到它的时间通常与后台作业有关，因为这些作业已经将它们的数据存储在 Redis 中（至少在我从事过的大多数应用程序中）并且通常也是临时的。 在这种情况下，将相关数据（例如完成百分比）存储在具有相似水平的短期持久性的同一位置似乎很自然。

      与所有“不走寻常路”的事物一样，您应该警惕将此类事物引入您的代码库。 我建议至少添加一些注释来向未来的开发人员解释为什么您使用他们可能不熟悉的这种方法。



原文:

This article was originally written by Jonathan Miles on the Honeybadger Developer Blog.

Rails is a large framework with a lot of handy tools built-in for specific situations. In this series, we'll take a look at some of the lesser-known tools hidden in Rails' large codebase.

In this article, we'll explain the increment and decrement methods in Rails.cache.

The Rails.cache Helper

Rails.cache is the entryway to interact with the cache in your application. It's also an abstraction, giving you a common API to call regardless of the actual cache "store" being used under the hood. Out of the box, Rails supports the following:

  • FileStore
  • MemoryStore
  • MemCacheStore
  • NullStore
  • RedisCacheStore

Inspecting Rails.cache will show which one you are running:

> Rails.cache
=> <#ActiveSupport::Cache::RedisCacheStore options={:namespace=>nil, ...

We won't look at them all in detail, but as a quick summary:

  • NullStore does not store anything; reading back from this will always return nil. This is the default for a new Rails app.
  • FileStore stores the cache as files on the hard drive, so they persist even if you restart your Rails server.
  • MemoryStore keeps the cache in RAM, so if you stop your Rails server, you will also wipe the cache.
  • MemCacheStore and RedisCacheStore use external programs (MemCache and Redis, respectively) to maintain the cache.

For various reasons, the first three here are used most often for development/testing. In production, you would likely use Redis or MemCache.

Because Rails.cache abstracts away the differences between these services, it is easy to run different versions in different environments without changing the code; for example, you could use NullStore in development, MemoryStore in testing, and RedisCacheStore in production.

Cached Data

Through Rails' Rails.cache.read, Rails.cache.write, and Rails.cache.fetch, we have an easy way to store and retrieve any arbitrary data from the cache. An earlier article covered these in more detail; the important thing to note for this article is that there is no in-built thread-safety on these methods. Let’s say we are updating the cached data from multiple threads to keep a running count; we will need to wrap the read/write operation in some kind of lock to avoid race conditions. Consider this example, assuming we have set things up to use a Redis cache store:

threads = []
# Set initial counter
Rails.cache.write(:test_counter, 0) 4.times do threads << Thread.new do 100.times do current_count = Rails.cache.read(:test_counter) current_count += 1 Rails.cache.write(:test_counter, current_count) end end
end threads.map(&:join) puts Rails.cache.read(:test_counter)

Here we have four threads, each one incrementing our cached value one hundred times. The result should be 400, but most of the time, it will be much less - 269 on my test run. What's happening here is a race condition. I covered these in more detail in the previous article, but as a quick summary, because the threads are all operating on the same "shared" data, they can get out of sync with each other. For example, one thread might read the value, then another thread takes over and reads the value as well, increments it, and stores it. The first thread then resumes, using its now-out-of-date value.

The common way to solve this problem is to surround the code with a mutually exclusive lock (or Mutex) so that only one thread can execute code within the lock at a time. In our case, though, Rails.cache has some methods to handle this scenario.

Rails Cache Increment and Decrement

The Rails.cache object has both increment and decrement methods for acting directly on cached data like our counter scenario:

 threads = [] # Set initial counter Rails.cache.write(:test_counter, 0, raw: true) 4.times do threads << Thread.new do 100.times do Rails.cache.increment(:test_counter) # repeating the increment just to highlight the thread safety Rails.cache.decrement(:test_counter) Rails.cache.increment(:test_counter) end end end threads.map(&:join) puts Rails.cache.read(:test_counter, raw: true)

To use increment and decrement, we have to tell the cache store it is a 'raw' value (via raw: true). You have to do this when reading the value back as well; otherwise, you'll get an error. Basically, we are telling the cache we want this value stored as a bare integer so that we can call increment/decrement on it, but you can still use expires_in and other cache flags at the same time.

The key here is that increment and decrement use atomic operations (at least for Redis and MemCache), meaning they are thread safe; there is no way for a thread to pause during an atomic operation.

It's worth noting, although I haven't used it in my example here, that both methods also return the new value. Therefore, if you need to use the new counter value beyond just updating it, you can also do so without an additional read call.

Real-World Applications

On the face of it, these increment and decrement methods seem like low-level helper methods, the kind you probably only care about if you are implementing or maintaining something like a background job processing gem. Once you know about them, though, you may be surprised where they can come in handy.

I've used these in a production application to avoid duplicate scheduled background jobs running simultaneously. In our case, we have various scheduled jobs to update search indices, mark abandoned carts, and so on. Generally, this works fine; the catch is that some jobs (search indices in particular) consume a lot of memory - enough that, if two were to run together, it would exceed our Heroku dyno's limit, and the worker would be killed off. Because we have a few of these jobs, it is not as simple as marking them no-retries or forcing unique jobs; two different (and thus unique) jobs could try to run at the same time and bring down the worker.

To prevent this, we created a base class for the scheduled jobs that keeps a counter of how many are currently running. If the count is too high, the job simply re-enqueues itself and waits.

Another example was on a side-project of mine where a background job (or multiple jobs) do some processing while the user has to wait. This brings up the common issue of communicating the current progress to the user of the background job. While there are numerous ways this could be solved, as an experiment, I tried using Rails.cache.increment to update a globally available counter. The structure was as follows:

  1. First, I added a new class in /app/models to abstract away the fact that the counter lived in the cache. This is where all access to the value would flow through. Part of this is generating the unique cache key related to the job.
  2. The job then creates an instance of this model and updates it as items are processed.
  3. A simple JSON endpoint creates an instance of this model to grab the current value.
  4. The front-end polls this endpoint every few seconds to update the UI. You could, of course, make this fancier with something like ActionCable and push out updates.

Conclusion

Honestly, Rails.cache.increment is not a tool I would reach for often, as it is not often that I want to update data stored in the cache (which is, by nature, somewhat temporary). As noted above, the times I do reach for it are generally related to background jobs since the jobs are already storing their data in Redis (at least in most apps I've worked on) and are also generally temporary. In such cases, it seems natural to store related data (percentage complete, for example) in the same place with a similar level of short-term persistence.

As with all things "off the beaten path", you should be wary of introducing things like this into your codebase. I'd recommend, at least, adding some comments to explain to future developers why you are using this method that they are probably not familiar with.

O Github é cheio de funcionalidades e o mais utilizado isso vocês já sabem e vamos continuar a série de "hacks" da plataforma que vão otimizar e melhorar sua experiência.

Atalhos sempre agilizam o trabalho com qualquer ferramenta para isso esse post veio mostrar alguns atalhos interessantes do Github.

Buscando arquivos

Quando visualizamos um repositório, podemos pressionar t e começar a digitar um nome de arquivo que contenha nesse diretório, que o arquivo será buscado recursivamente em todos os diretórios:

Branch selector

Agora quando trabalhamos com várias branchs, podemos usar o w, na tela principal do repositório, para abrir o Branch Selector. O campo de busca de branchs receberá foco.

Command bar

Labels

Todos os atalhos

Items.Title: CSS 选择器 | CSS selector
Items.Description:
什么是选择器

选择器的类型
有些是：

  1. 通用选择器
  2. 类型选择器
  3. 类选择器
  4. 标识选择器
  5. 子选择器
  6. 后代选择器
  7. 相邻兄弟选择器

让我们详细阅读：

通用选择器

通用选择器是 CSS 中的 *。 字面意思是星号字符。 它本质上是一个匹配任何类型的类型选择器。 类型表示 HTML 标记，如<div> 、 <body> 、 <p>或任何其他标记。

一个常见的用途是通用复位，如下所示：

 * { margin : 0 ; padding : 0 ; }

例子：

类型选择器

类型选择器选择具有给定节点名称的所有 HTML 元素。 例如，“a”将选择所有<a>元素并将 CSS 属性值应用于它们。 您还可以使用定义的命名空间将类型选择器限制为仅在该命名空间内的元素。

句法：

 elementname { /* CSS property */ }

例子：

类选择器

为了选择具有特定类的元素，请使用句点 (.) 字符指定类名，即，它将根据其类属性的内容匹配 HTML 元素。 类名主要用于将 CSS 属性设置为给定的类。

句法：

 .class { /*CSS property*/ }

例子：

标识选择器

在样式表文档中，id 选择器的名称前面有一个“#”。 如果一个 id 选择器与另一个或多个选择器组合，则 id 选择器必须以 # 开头。 句法：

 #id { /* CSS property */ }

例子

子选择器

如果x、y和z是三个HTML元素，z位于y的开始和结束标记内，y位于x的开始和结束标记内； 然后 y 被称为 x 的孩子； 在编写子选择器时，选择器必须用“>”组合符分隔。

句法：

 parent > child { /* CSS property*/ }

例子：

后代选择器

它结合了两个选择器，其中第一个选择器代表一个祖先（父级、父级的父级等），第二个选择器代表后代。 如果第二个选择器匹配的元素具有与第一个选择器匹配的祖先元素，则它们将被选中。 后代选择器使用后代组合符。

句法

selector1 selector2 { /* CSS property */ }

例子：

相邻兄弟选择器

匹配紧跟在另一个元素之后的元素，但不是它的子元素。

例如，如果我们希望紧跟在h4之后的所有段落都具有绿色文本，而不是其他段落，我们将使用以下 CSS 规则：

句法

selector1 + selector2 { /* property declarations */ }

例子：



原文:

What are selectors

A selectors in CSS is a part of the CSS ruleset, that is basically used to select the element you want to style. CSS selectors select HTML elements according to their id, class, type, attribute, etc.

Types of Selectors
There are various types of selectors in CSS. Some are:

  1. Universal Selector
  2. Type Selector
  3. Class Selector
  4. Id Selector
  5. Child Selector
  6. Descendant Selector
  7. Adjacent Sibling Selector

Let's read about in details:

Universal Selector

The Universal Selector is the * in CSS. Literally the asterisk character. It is essentially a type selector that matches any type. Type meaning an HTML tag like <div>, <body>, <p> or any of the others tag.

A common use is in the universal reset, like this:

* { margin: 0; padding: 0;
}

Example:

Type Selector

A type selector selects all HTML elements that have a given node name. For example, “a” would select all <a> elements and apply the CSS property values to them. “Input” would select all <input> elements, “span” all <span> elements and so on.

You can also use a defined namespace to restrict the type selector to elements only within that namespace.

Syntax:

elementname{ /* CSS property */
}

Example:

Class Selector

The .class selector is used to select all elements which belong to a particular class attribute. In order to select the elements with a particular class, use the period (.) character specifying the class name ie., it will match the HTML element based on the contents of their class attribute. The class name is mostly used to set the CSS property to a given class.

Syntax:

.class { /*CSS property*/
}

Example:

Id Selector

CSS id selectors select any element in an HTML page, if it has an attribute called id, whose value matches the name of the id selector.
In a stylesheet document, the name of the id selector is preceded by a "#". If an id selector is combined with another or more selectors, id selectors must be preceded with a #. IDs should be unique in an HTML page i.e., same Id can't be given to multiple elements on a single HTML page.

Syntax:

#id { /* CSS property */
}

Example

Child Selector

CSS child selectors select an element which is a child of another element.

If, x, y and z are three HTML elements and z resides within start and end tag of y, and y resides within start and end tag of x; then y is called as a child of x; and z is called as a child of y.

 <x> <y> <z></z> </y> </x>

While writing child selectors, selectors must be separated with ">" combinator.

syntax:

parent > child{ /* CSS property*/
}

Example:

Descendant Selector

The Descendant combinator is represented using a single space. It combines two selectors in which the first selector represents an ancestor (parent, parent's parent, etc.), and the second selector represents descendants. The elements matched by the second selector are selected if they have an ancestor element that matches the first selector. Descendant selectors use the descendant combinators.

Syntax

selector1 selector2 { /* CSS property */ }

Example:

Adjacent Sibling Selector

Match an element that is immediately after another element, but not a child of it.

For example, if we wanted all paragraphs that immediately followed an h4 to have green text, but not other paragraphs, we would use the following CSS rule:

Syntax

selector1 + selector2 { /* property declarations */ }

Example:

Items.Title: 如何创建 Slack 机器人 | How to Create a Slack Bot
Items.Description:
如果您想创建高度自定义和强大的 Slack 体验，这确实是最好的方法。 今天，我们将创建一个Slack Bot ，使用由 Next.js 构建的无服务器函数提供支持的 Web API 和 webhook 向各种对话发送消息。

关于 Vercel 和 Next.js 的一个小说明

使用 Web API 和 webhook 的好处在于，您可以选择自己的技术堆栈来构建您的 Slack Bot。 使用 Vercel，您可以部署无服务器功能，这些功能可以处理一系列事情，例如用户身份验证、数据库查询、自定义命令等。

创建 Slack 应用程序

首先，我们需要创建一个 Slack 应用程序。 您可以一直跟进，直到我们启动并运行 Slack 机器人。 单击此处创建您的应用程序。 从那里，我们可以选择我们希望如何配置应用程序的范围和设置。 随意做其中任何一个，如果你想加快这个过程，这里有一个应用程序清单，可以让你快速启动和运行。

我建议先尝试开发工作区！ 明智地选择您的工作空间和渠道！ 有关创建 Slack 工作区的更多信息，请参见此处。

发布您的第一条消息

我们今天要尝试的第一件事是发布您的第一条 Slack Bot 消息。 在能够对此进行测试之前，我们需要 (1) 配置我们的应用程序范围，并且我们需要 (2) 在我们的工作区中安装我们的应用程序。 我们可以通过我们应用的OAuth 和 Permissions来实现。

为了确定我们需要什么范围，让我们先回顾一下 API 方法。 此方法称为chat.postMessage ，此处是其文档的链接。 参考文档声明如下：

  • 端点： https://slack.com/api/chat.postMessage ://slack.com/api/chat.postMessage
  • 所需范围： chat:write

每次我们选择使用特定的 API 方法时，我们都需要确保我们的应用程序启用了所需的范围。 现在，让我们将chat:write和chat:write.public到我们的应用程序范围，并将我们的应用程序安装到我们的工作区。

即使文档不要求允许我们的 Slack 机器人将消息发送到他/她不是成员的频道， chat:write.public也会派上用场。

一旦我们完成了这两个步骤（定义范围和安装），我们应该获得一个机器人用户 OAuth 令牌（可从我们应用的 OAuth & Permissions 页面访问，并以xoxb- ）。 在 Slack 能够将其发布到我们的工作区之前，我们需要传递此令牌以验证我们的消息。

关于使用令牌的说明

将访问令牌传递给 Slack 的最佳方式是将它们传递到我们请求的Authorization HTTP 标头中，其中完整值（包括“Bearer”）区分大小写，应如下所示：

 POST /api/chat.postMessage Authorization: Bearer xoxb-1234-abcdefgh

必需的参数

随着我们不断查看 API 方法的文档，我们还看到，为了让 Slack 机器人发布消息，我们至少还需要声明一个channel和一个text 。 使用这个 cURL 片段（记得使用你的 Bot 令牌）和 Postman 试一试：

 curl --request POST 'https://slack.com/api/chat.postMessage' \ --header 'Content-Type: application/json' \ --header 'Authorization: Bearer xoxb-1234-abcdefgh' \ --data-raw '{ "channel": "general", "text": "Hello world :tada:" }'

注意：将general作为通道传递不被认为是一种好的做法。 这仅用于测试目的。 理想情况下，我们希望为channel声明一个channel_id而不是一个channel_name 。 以下是有关如何确保选择正确对话的更多信息（请记住记住您可能需要的其他范围）。

响应斜杠命令

斜线命令允许用户通过在消息框（即/greet ）中键入字符串来调用您的机器人。 它们还包含一个数据负载，您的 Slack 机器人可以使用这些数据以它们处理该负载的任何方式进行响应。 如果您想查看可能的内容，这里是 Slack 内置斜杠命令的列表。

从斜杠命令中，您可以单击创建新命令，这就是您需要的（这是我的一个示例）：

  • 命令： /greet
  • 请求网址： https://{...}.vercel.app/api/greet
  • 简短描述： Greet Mr. Meeseeks!

当你的命令被调用时会发生什么？

此请求包含描述源命令的数据有效负载，这是预期的示例（使用我们的/greet示例命令，更多关于每个字段的信息在此处）：

 { "token" : "{...}" , "team_id" : "{...}" , "team_domain" : "{...}" , "channel_id" : "{...}" , "channel_name" : "{...}" , "user_id" : "{...}" , "user_name" : "{...}" , "command" : "/greet" , "text" : "" , "api_app_id" : "{...}" , "is_enterprise_install" : "{...}" , "response_url" : "https://hooks.slack.com/commands/{...}" , "trigger_id" : "{...}"
}

回应用户

响应用户时有多个选项可供选择（请参阅此处的所有选项）。 让我们写一个常规的Message response 。 要编写一个，从我们的 Slash Command 有效负载中，我们将使用以下字段来编写消息： user_id 、 user_name 、 response_url 。 对于内容块，让我们使用他们的Block Kit Builder为我们的消息设置样式，使用以下有效负载：

 { "blocks" : [ { "type" : "section" , "text" : { "type" : "mrkdwn" , "text" : "Hello, <@${user_id}>! I'm Mr. Meeseeks! Look at me!" } } ]
}

当您为用户构建更好的交互性时，请随意尝试使用它。 这里还有一个很棒的指南，介绍如何为您的应用程序设置文本格式。

那么，我们如何构建我们的响应呢？ 为此，我们需要启动一个 Next.js 项目。 如果您需要帮助来启动一个，我建议您使用此资源。 在那里，让我们创建一个名为greet的 API 文件，使用.env.local文件设置我们的Bot User OAuth Token环境变量（在此处阅读有关环境变量的更多信息）。 这是我最后写的：

 export default async function handler () { const { response_url , user_id } = req . body ; try { const headers = { Authorization : `Bearer ${ process . env . BOT_TOKEN } ` , " Content-type " : " application/json " , }; let raw = `{ response_type: "in_channel", blocks: [ { type: "section", text: { type: "mrkdwn", text: "Hello, <@ ${ user_id } >! I'm Mr. Meeseeks! Look at me!", }, }, ], text: "Hello, I'm Mr. Meeseeks! Look at me!", }` ; const requestOptions = { method : " POST " , headers , body : raw , }; await fetch ( ` ${ response_url } ` , requestOptions ); res . status ( 200 ). end (); } catch ( error ) { console . log ( error ); } }

在这里，我添加了多个 Slash 命令：

用你的机器人探索提及

让我们探索另一个非常常见的机器人用例：处理提及。 事件 API是一种简化的、简单的方法来构建响应 Slack 中的活动的机器人。

要开始使用事件，请找到“事件订阅”配置页面并使用切换开关将其打开。 触发事件时 Slack 将发送HTTP POST请求的请求 URL 将是必需的。

数字握手

发送到您的请求 URL 的事件可能包含与批准您的 Slack 应用程序的工作区相关的敏感信息。 为确保将事件传送到您直接控制的服务器，我们必须通过向您发出质询请求来验证您的所有权。

您的应用程序需要的第一件事是一个请求 URL，Slack 将在其中发送一个不需要身份验证的 HTTP Post 请求，但您需要有一个HTTP 200 OK的服务器响应代码并返回质询。 这是我如何实现我的：

 const { challenge } = req . body ; res . status ( 200 ). json ({ challenge : ` ${ challenge } ` });

验证您的 URL 后，继续选择您要注册的活动。 我要和app_mention一起去。 还要验证您的机器人是否具有您注册的事件所需的范围。 在这种情况下app_mentions:read是必需的。 这是您可以期待的基本事件结构有效负载：

 { token: ' { .. } ' , team_id: ' { .. } ' , api_app_id: ' { .. } ' , event: { client_msg_id: ' { .. } ' , type: 'app_mention' , text: '<@U 03 JZTCSEC 8 >' , user: ' { .. } ' , ts: ' { .. } ' , team: ' { .. } ' , blocks: [ [ Object ] ], channel: ' { .. } ' , event_ts: ' { .. } ' }, type: 'event_callback' , event_id: ' { .. } ' , event_time: 1654874099 , authorizations: [ { enterprise_id: null , team_id: ' { .. } ' , user_id: ' { .. } ' , is_bot: true , is_enterprise_install: false } ], is_ext_shared_channel: false , event_context: ' { .. } '
}

然后，一旦我确定我想如何处理这个事件。 我相应地处理我的处理程序函数。 请注意，事件 API 没有像斜杠命令那样的response_url ，因此请考虑到这一点。 此外app_mentions类型事件仅适用于频道中的提及，无论是邀请提及还是您的 Slack 机器人的后续提及。

结论

如果您与朋友或在工作中拥有 Slack Workspace，您绝对可以尝试一下Meeseeks 。 这个 Slack Bot 是开源和公开分发的（除非 Slack 在审查后将其删除）。 有关更多详细信息和信息，您可以参考Meeseeks GitHub 存储库，因为它是开源的。

如果 Meeseeks 是你的机器人，你会为他构建什么？



原文:

Slack applications have full access to its platform. It's really the best way to go if you want to create a highly custom and powerful Slack experience. Slack offers a large range of APIs that provide access to read, write, and update all kinds of data in Slack.

Today we will create a Slack Bot to send messages to all kinds of conversations using Web APIs and webhooks powered by Serverless Functions built using Next.js.

A small note on Vercel and Next.js

The great thing about working with Web APIs and webhooks is that you can choose your own Tech Stack to build your Slack Bot. Next.js has support for API Routes, which lets you easily create an API endpoint as a Node.js serverless function. With Vercel, you can deploy serverless functions, that handle an array of things such as user authentication, database queries, custom commands and more.

Creating a Slack app

First, we need to create a Slack app. You can follow along all the way until we get our Slack bot up and running. Click here to create your application. From there, we can choose how we'd like to configure our app's scopes and settings. Feel free to do either of them, in case you want to speed up the process, here's an app manifest to get you up and running quickly.

I'd recommend experimenting with a Development Workspace first! Choose your workspace and channels wisely! For more on Creating a Slack Workspace see here.

Posting your first Message

The first thing we will try today is posting your first Slack Bot message. Before being able to test this, we need to (1) configure our application Scopes and we need to (2) install our application in our Workspace. We can do both from our app's OAuth & Permissions.

In order to determine what scopes do we need, let's review the API method first. This method is called chat.postMessage, here a link to its docs. The reference docs declare the following:

  • HTTP Method: POST
  • Endpoint: https://slack.com/api/chat.postMessage
  • Required scopes: chat:write

Every time we choose to use a specific API Method, we need to make sure our app has the required scopes enabled. For now, let's add chat:write and chat:write.public to our application Scopes and install our application to our Workspace.

chat:write.public will come in handy even if it isn't required by the docs to allow our Slack bot to send messages to channels where he/she isn't a member of.

Once we have done these two steps (defining scopes and installing), we should get an Bot User OAuth Token (accessible from our app's OAuth & Permissions page and starts with xoxb-). We need to pass this token in order to authenticate our message before Slack is able to post it to our Workspace.

A note on using tokens

The best way to communicate your access tokens to Slack is by passing them in our request's Authorization HTTP header where the full value, including "Bearer", is case-sensitive and should look something like this:

POST /api/chat.postMessage
Authorization: Bearer xoxb-1234-abcdefgh

Required arguments

As we keep reviewing our API method's docs, we also see that in order for our Slack bot to post a message, we also need at least to declare a channel and a text. Use this cURL snippet (remember to use your Bot's token) and Postman to give it a try:

curl --request POST 'https://slack.com/api/chat.postMessage' \
--header 'Content-Type: application/json' \
--header 'Authorization: Bearer xoxb-1234-abcdefgh' \
--data-raw '{ "channel": "general", "text": "Hello world :tada:"
}'

Note: passing general as the channel isn't considered a good practice. This is only for testing purposes. Ideally we want to declare a channel_id instead of a channel_name for channel. Here's more on how you can make sure you are picking the right conversation (remember to keep in mind additional scopes you may require).

Responding to Slash Commands

Slash Commands allow users to invoke your bot by typing a string into the message box (i.e. /greet). They also contain a payload of data that your Slack bot can use to respond in whatever way they process that payload. Here is a list of Slack built-in slash commands if you want to review what's possible.

From Slash Commands you are able to click on Create a New Command and here's what you'll need (here's an example of mine):

  • Command: /greet
  • Request URL: https://{...}.vercel.app/api/greet
  • Short Description: Greet Mr. Meeseeks!

What happens when your command is invoked?

Slack sends an HTTP POST to the Request URL you specific in your command. This request contains a data payload describing the source command, here's an example of what to expect (using our /greet example command, more about each field here):

{ "token":"{...}", "team_id":"{...}", "team_domain":"{...}", "channel_id":"{...}", "channel_name":"{...}", "user_id":"{...}", "user_name":"{...}", "command":"/greet", "text":"", "api_app_id":"{...}", "is_enterprise_install":"{...}", "response_url":"https://hooks.slack.com/commands/{...}", "trigger_id":"{...}"
}

Responding to users

There are several options to choose from when responding to a user (see all options here). Let's write a regular Message response. To write one, from our Slash Command payload, we'll use the following fields to compose a message: user_id, user_name, response_url. Slack lets you write messages in Content Blocks and in plain text. For Content Blocks let's style our message using their Block Kit Builder, using the following Payload:

{ "blocks": [ { "type": "section", "text": { "type": "mrkdwn", "text": "Hello, <@${user_id}>! I'm Mr. Meeseeks! Look at me!" } } ]
}

Feel free to get experimental with using it as you build a better interactivity for your users. Here's also a great Guide on how to format your text for your application.

Next.js API Handler

So, how are we building our response? For that we need to fire up a Next.js project. If you need help starting one, I'd recommend using this resource. Once there, let's create an API file named greet, set up our Bot User OAuth Token environment variable using an .env.local file (read more about environment variables here). Here's what I ended up writing:

export default async function handler() { const { response_url, user_id } = req.body; try { const headers = { Authorization: `Bearer ${process.env.BOT_TOKEN}`, "Content-type": "application/json", }; let raw = `{ response_type: "in_channel", blocks: [ { type: "section", text: { type: "mrkdwn", text: "Hello, <@${user_id}>! I'm Mr. Meeseeks! Look at me!", }, }, ], text: "Hello, I'm Mr. Meeseeks! Look at me!", }`; const requestOptions = { method: "POST", headers, body: raw, }; await fetch(`${response_url}`, requestOptions); res.status(200).end(); } catch (error) { console.log(error); }
}

Here what I've done adding multiple Slash commands:

Exploring Mentions with your Bot

Let's explore another highly common use case for your bot: Handling mentions. Slacks enables mentions via the subscription of events. The Events API is a streamlined, easy way to build bots that respond to activities in Slack.

To begin working with Events, find the "Event Subscriptions" configuration page and use the toggle to turn them on. A Request URL will be required where Slack will send HTTP POST requests when the event is triggered.

Digital Handshake

The events sent to your Request URL may contain sensitive information associated with the workspaces having approved your Slack app. To ensure that events are being delivered to a server under your direct control, we must verify your ownership by issuing you a challenge request.

The first thing your application will require is a Request URL where Slack will send an HTTP Post request that doesn't require Authentication but you need to have a server respond code of HTTP 200 OK and return the challenge as well. Here's how I implemented mine:

const { challenge } = req.body;
res.status(200).json({ challenge: `${challenge}` });

Once your URL is verified, go ahead and select an event that you wish to register to. I'm gonna go with app_mention. Also verify that your bot has the required scopes for the event you registered. In this case app_mentions:read is required. Here's the basic event structure payload you can expect:

{ token: '{..}', team_id: '{..}', api_app_id: '{..}', event: { client_msg_id: '{..}', type: 'app_mention', text: '<@U03JZTCSEC8>', user: '{..}', ts: '{..}', team: '{..}', blocks: [ [Object] ], channel: '{..}', event_ts: '{..}' }, type: 'event_callback', event_id: '{..}', event_time: 1654874099, authorizations: [ { enterprise_id: null, team_id: '{..}', user_id: '{..}', is_bot: true, is_enterprise_install: false } ], is_ext_shared_channel: false, event_context: '{..}'
}

Then, once I identify how I would like to handle the event. I process my handler function accordingly. Note that the Event API doesn't have a response_url as the Slash Command does, so take that into account. Also app_mentions type events only apply for Mentions in Channels whether it is the invite mention or subsequent mentions of your Slack Bot.

Conclusion

If you have a Slack Workspace with your friends or at work, you can definitely give Meeseeks a try. This Slack Bot is opened sourced and publicly distributed (unless Slack takes it down after its review). For more details and information you can reference the Meeseeks GitHub repository since it is Open-sourced.

If Meeseeks were your bot, what would you build for him?

Items.Title: 为你的下一个大项目考虑这 8 个令人惊叹的 React 组件库（我是认真的） | Consider These 8 Amazing React Component Libraries for Your Next Big Project (I mean it)
Items.Description:
对于 Web 开发人员来说，制作可访问的用户界面通常既困难又耗时。 当我们必须从头开始创建特定组件（例如 Modals 或 Popovers）时，这一点会被放大。

幸运的是，React 生态系统非常庞大，许多优秀的人创建了令人难以置信的框架来帮助我们应对这一挑战。 今天，我们将看看 React 组件库，它们易于使用、具有强大的基本风格、充足的文档，并包含 Modals、Popovers、Tooltips 等组件。 给我买杯咖啡

我能够在很短的时间内创建非常复杂的 UI（带有模式和表格以及所有内容），这使我能够专注于其他事情，例如应用程序逻辑。 它非常适合在黑客马拉松中使用！ 它还拥有一个庞大而活跃的社区，并且非常受欢迎。

以下 UI 可能是本文中五个 UI 中最具视觉吸引力的。 尽管它仍处于测试阶段，但它配备了您需要的一切，而且开箱即用，看起来很棒！ 它还包括一些开箱即用的令人难以置信的过渡和动画，这是其他组件库所没有的。

它基于谷歌的材料设计，但也允许大量定制。 其中一些组件是免费的，而另一些则需要支付许可费。

它类似于 Chakra UI，但社区较小。 但是，它确实提供了一些很棒的功能，例如通知中心、命令栏、富文本编辑器等等！

它带有大量开箱即用的主题和组件。 使用 Tailwind CSS 进行定制相对简单，因为它是基于它构建的，并且是随它一起提供的。

作为企业级产品的设计系统，AntD 有别于其他 React 组件库。 给我买杯咖啡

蚂蚁金服、阿里巴巴、腾讯和百度都是采用 AntD 设计理念的知名公司。 它与其他 React 框架的区别在于“旨在为桌面应用程序构建复杂的数据密集界面”。 鉴于 Blueprint 与 Palantir 的联系，这不足为奇。

它包含 90 多个出色的 React 组件，您可以立即在项目中使用它们。 该库建立在与设计无关的框架上，允许您使用当前流行的库，如 Material 和 Bootstrap，或创建自己的库。 它带有自己的主题设计器，它是基于 GUI 的，包含 500 多个可供使用的变量。

关闭

如果您有任何其他收藏夹或这五个中的哪一个是您最喜欢的，请告诉我！

给我买杯咖啡



原文:

Making accessible user interfaces is often difficult and time-consuming for web developers. This is amplified when we have to create specific components from scratch, such as Modals or Popovers.

Fortunately, the React ecosystem is vast, and many wonderful people have created incredible frameworks to assist us with this challenge. Today, we'll look at React component libraries that are easy to use, have a strong basic style, adequate documentation, and include components like Modals, Popovers, Tooltips, and so on. Buy me a coffee

1) Chakra User Interface

Chakra UI was the first component library I used when I initially started using Next.js, and it was fantastic! I was able to create very complicated UIs (with modals and tables and everything) in a short amount of time, which allowed me to focus on other things, such as application logic. It is ideal for usage at hackathons! It also has a large and active community and is incredibly popular.

2) NEXT UI

The following UI is perhaps the most visually appealing of the five in this post. Although it is still in the beta stage, it comes with everything you need and looks fantastic right out of the box! It also includes some incredible transitions and animations out of the box, which other component libraries do not.

3) MUI

MUI, formerly known as Material UI, has also been around for a long time. It is based on Google's Material Design, but it also allows for substantial customisation. MUI also includes an unstyled version and a package including some fantastic CSS tools. MUI X additionally has a range of sophisticated components. Some of these components are free, while others need a license fee.

4) Mantine

Mantine also includes a large number of components and a functional UI out of the box. It's similar to Chakra UI, but with a smaller community. It does, however, provide some fantastic features like as a notification center, a command bar, a rich text editor, and much more!

5) React Daisy UI

Daisy UI is an incredible Tailwind CSS component library. Daisy UI is a Daisy UI component framework written in React. It comes with a large variety of themes and components out of the box. It is relatively simple to customize using Tailwind CSS because it is built on it and comes with it.

6) Ant Design (AntD)

Ant Design (also known as AntD) bills itself as "the world's second most popular React UI," while it's unclear who they consider to be the most popular. As a design system for enterprise-level products, AntD distinguishes itself from other React component libraries. AntD also has a design philosophy based on four values: natural, certain, meaningful, and growing. Buy me a coffee

Ant Financial, Alibaba, Tencent, and Baidu are among the notable firms that have embraced AntD's design philosophy. Ant Design was developed by Ant Financial and introduced in 2016—more on this later.

7) Blueprint

Palantir's Blueprint is an open-source React UI package. It distinguishes itself from other React frameworks by being "designed for constructing sophisticated data-dense interfaces for desktop apps." Given Blueprint's Palantir connections, this comes as no surprise.

Blueprint splits component libraries based on use cases and important dependencies in addition to its core component package.

8) PrimeReact

PrimeReact is a huge React component library utilized by large organizations all around the world.

It includes over 90 outstanding React components that you can use right away in your project. TreeSelect, Organization chart, Terminal, and Captcha are some of the distinctive features it provides. PrimeReact also includes highly adjustable templates and 280+ ready-to-paste UI components to let you quickly create any interface.

The library is built on a design-agnostic framework and allows you to use current popular libraries such as Material and Bootstrap, or create your own. It comes with its own Theme Designer, which is GUI-based and contains 500+ variables to play with.

Closure

That concludes the list of 5 React component libraries that can help you speed up development. Please let me know if you have any additional favorites or which of these five was your favorite!

Buy me a coffee
